preamble: The elision of the voiced uvular stop [ɢ] is a common path in the lenition and eventual loss of dorsal obstruents, often passing through a fricative stage [ʁ].

examples.note: Historical voiced uvular stops have elided in some positions or shifted significantly in North African dialects.
Development of back-articulated stops into zero, often with compensatory lengthening of preceding vowels.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
